name: Lécera - Moneva - Moyuela - Azuara — circular from Letux
name_original: Lécera - Moneva - Moyuela - Azuara — circular desde Letux
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Letux traverse the Campo de Belchite region, characterized by expansive, often flat, steppe terrains and cereal fields. This landscape provides opportunities for long, smooth rides through open countryside. The broader region also features varied topography, including gullies, gorges, and small canyons, which introduce gradients and scenic challenges for road cyclists. The Aguasvivas River contributes to the natural features, offering diverse landscapes along its course. Best road cycling routes around Letux The most popular road cycling route is Lécera - Moneva - Moyuela - Azuara — circular from Letux, a 38.1 miles (61.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 41 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular journey through the open countryside of the region. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Belchite, Codo and Almonacid, a moderate 23.5 miles (37.8 km) path. This route connects historical sites and offers varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Local road cyclists also love the Tosos – Herrera de los Navarros loop from Azuara, a 56.2 miles (90.4 km) trail leading through varied geological formations, often completed in about 4 hours 22 minutes. Road cycling around Letux is defined by steppe landscapes, extensive cereal fields, and river oases. The network offers options for different ability levels, from flat, open roads to routes with more significant gradients. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Letux's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of road cycling routes can I find around Letux?
A: The region around Letux offers a diverse range of road cycling routes. You'll find expansive, often flat, steppe terrains with cereal fields, perfect for long, smooth rides. For those seeking more challenge, the broader Campo de Belchite region features varied topography, including gullies, gorges, and small canyons, introducing interesting gradients and scenic challenges.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families near Letux?
A: Yes, the immediate vicinity of Letux features predominantly flat terrain, making it accessible for various cycling levels, including leisurely rides suitable for beginners. While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly detailed, the flatter sections provide a good starting point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992591032/lecera-moneva-moyuela-azuara-circular-from-letux" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lécera - Moneva - Moyuela - Azuara — circular from Letux&lt;/a&gt; route is moderate and offers a circular journey through open countryside.

Q: What are some of the more challenging road cycling routes in the Letux area?
A: For experienced road cyclists looking for a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31884285" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tosos – Herrera de los Navarros loop from Azuara&lt;/a&gt; offer significant gradients and varied geological formations over 90.4 km. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150212" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrera de los Navarros – Moneva loop from Empalme Almonacid&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 126.4 km with substantial elevation changes.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along road cycling routes near Letux?
A: Road cycling routes in the region often pass by significant historical sites. You can explore the poignant ruins of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Village of Belchite&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5268320" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Dam of Almonacid de la Cuba&lt;/a&gt;, noted as one of the highest built during the Roman Empire. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991339238/belchite-codo-and-almonacid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belchite, Codo and Almonacid&lt;/a&gt; specifically connects several historical points.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its unique steppe landscapes, characterized by brownish-grey and ochre colors, and extensive cereal fields that provide a serene backdrop. The Aguasvivas River also offers natural beauty, including features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3592739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza de los Chorros&lt;/a&gt; (a series of small waterfalls). Many routes offer scenic viewpoints over these diverse landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling around Letux?
A: The region experiences a continental Mediterranean climate, with hot, dry summers and cold winters. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the intense summer heat and potential winter chill. These seasons also showcase the changing colors of the steppe and cereal fields.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available from Letux?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992591032/lecera-moneva-moyuela-azuara-circular-from-letux" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lécera - Moneva - Moyuela - Azuara — circular from Letux&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a comprehensive tour of the open countryside and is a moderate 61.4 km ride. Another circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Village of Belchite – Old Village of Belchite loop from Belchite&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for road cycling routes in this area?
A: Road cycling routes around Letux vary significantly in length and duration. You can find moderate routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991339238/belchite-codo-and-almonacid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belchite, Codo and Almonacid&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 37.8 km and takes around 1 hour 40 minutes. For longer rides, routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150212" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrera de los Navarros – Moneva loop from Empalme Almonacid&lt;/a&gt; extend to over 126 km, requiring nearly 6 hours to complete.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Letux?
A: Road cyclists often praise the unique blend of open steppe landscapes and historical sites. The region offers opportunities for both smooth, fast rides through cereal fields and more challenging terrain with varied gradients. The ability to combine physical activity with cultural exploration, such as visiting the Old Village of Belchite, is a frequently highlighted aspect.

Q: Are there any towns or villages along the routes where I can find cafes or services?
A: Yes, many routes pass through or near small towns and villages where you can find amenities. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992591032/lecera-moneva-moyuela-azuara-circular-from-letux" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lécera - Moneva - Moyuela - Azuara — circular from Letux&lt;/a&gt; route connects several settlements like Lécera, Moneva, Moyuela, and Azuara, where you can typically find places to rest and refuel.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Letux?
A: There are 6 road cycling routes detailed around Letux, catering to various skill levels. This includes 1 easy route, 3 moderate routes, and 2 difficult routes, offering a good selection for different preferences.
tips: Located on the outskirts of the Zaragoza town of Letux, we find this beautiful baroque hermitage that, thanks to the contributions of the neighbors, was restored after the Spanish Civil War.
Declared a Cataloged Asset of Aragonese Cultural Heritage, the Church of Santa María Magdalena rises above the town of Lécera with a clearly Mudejar style.

The Church of Nuestra Señora de la Piedad was built in the second half of the 14th century in the style of a Mudejar fortified church, but it was modified in the 18th century. Now it is used for masses and is cared for by the 100 inhabitants. The 2 times that they have opened doors, the visitors have been open-mouthed with the interior.
